qas.q: Operations Security
5 Step OPSEC Process
qas.a: Critical Info; Threat; Vulnerability; Risk and Apply OPSEC measures (mitigation)

qas.q: 2 base components of Essential Secrecy
A category of OPSEC indicators is?
qas.a: SPACE (signature; profile; association; contrast and exposure)

qas.q: Good characteristics of a good CIL
qas.a: Unclassified; 10 items or less; widest dissemination

qas.q: Why conduct an OPSEC review?
qas.a: Evaluate compliance to regulatory guidance, prevent potential release of critical information; to evaluate a product or document with new eyes

qas.q: What is CALI?
What is RISK?
qas.a: The probability that an adversary will compromise your Critical Information and the resulting impact if successful

qas.q: In order to have a threat, you must have what two components?
What is VULNERABILITY?
qas.a: It is the action of an adversary after careful observation, and positioning in order to plan to effectively act against a friendly organization

qas.q: What is the regulation for SAP?
What is the Army Regulation for OPSEC?
When did Regan sign NSDD 298?
Simple version of the OPSEC 5 step process
qas.a: 1. problem, 2. it could get worse, 3. damage, 4. timing (odds of it getting worse), 5. mitigation...

qas.q: Essential Secrecy is?
qas.a:
The condition achieved from denial of critical information to adversaries through the combined efforts of:
a.) Traditional Security programs and
b.) the Operations Security process...

qas.q: CALI maintains our "Element of Surprise" by?
qas.a: Denying enemies information... It is a key part of Essential Secrecy

qas.q: Who owns the OPSEC program...?
Who determines what will and will not be protected?
What is a key responsibility/duty of the OPSEC officer?
Other responsibilities/duties of the OPSEC officer include?
qas.a:
1. Producing Official Policy Letter
2. Coordinating functions within a Working Group
3. Publishing an OPSEC SOP
4. Interagency Integration
5. Develop a CIL
6. Publish an Annual Report
7. Conduct/Promote OPSEC Training and Awareness
8. Conduct OPSEC Reviews
9. Take part in self/external program Assessments

qas.q: Critical Information comes from?
qas.a:
1. Protected Sources
2. Open Sources
3. Detectible Actions
NOTE: Intelligence Collection is not a Random Effort

qas.q: All OPSEC measures are implemented to?
Critical Information may be____________ or ____________ sensitive.
What is Threat driven?
What are some of the traditional Collection Methods?
qas.a: HUMINT; SIGINT; GEOINT; OSINT and MASINT

qas.q: Two types of HUMINT collection are?
Foreign Intelligence Services employ the following HUMINT methods?
Thinking like an Adversary collecting Critical Information UNDERSTAND and REMEMBER that...
Levels of Vulnerabilities exist at the?
qas.a:
1. Strategic Level, (S)
2. Operational Level, and the (O)
3. Tactical Level (T)

qas.q: Adversary analysts develop perceptions by?
qas.a:
1. Piecing the Indicators Together
2. Conducting a Comparative Analysis identifying Trends
3. Report Observations for Review Cand await Guidance

qas.q: 3 Categories of OPSEC Measures include?
Action Control measures are?
qas.a: Means of Mitigation, to include TRAINING...

qas.q: Countermeasures can look like?
Counter analysis can look like?
qas.a: Use of Deceptions / Decoys and False leads...

qas.q: A form of Risk Management is a Table Top Exercise, why is this employed?
qas.a: It helps leaders learn to make informed decisions regarding available courses of action.

qas.q: What is the OPSEC definition of Risk?
qas.a: The "probability" an adversary will compromise Critical Information and the "impact" is the severity of the damage taken when they are successful

qas.q: What does R = I x ( T x V ) mean?
qas.a: Risk equals Impact x threat x vulnerability

qas.q: What is the current risk without OPSEC measures
FOIA is?
qas.a: A public record. ANY official writing or recording.

DD Form 254

qas.a:
1. Awareness for the Cmd

qas.q: OPSEC Measures are?
Risk Management?
qas.a: Helps leaders make informed decisions regarding course of action

1. Offensive cyber ops
